summary: Drew DeVault announces sway 0.11, highlighting new security policy support for Wayland desktops and a bounty program to fund feature development. The project has grown to over 19,000 lines of C from 70 contributors and is packaged in multiple Linux distributions. Most core features are now implemented, with work shifting toward bug fixes, i3 compatibility improvements, and documentation. DeVault introduces a community bounty system seeded with $400 to incentivize new feature work across the sway ecosystem, and looks ahead to a 1.0 release in 2017.
key_points: Sway 0.11 introduces security policies to help realize Wayland's promise of a more secure desktop environment
The project has reached feature maturity with 70 contributors, 2,067 commits, and packages in major Linux distributions
A bounty program allows users to fund specific features and rewards contributors for implementing them, with over half the initial bounties targeting the broader ecosystem rather than sway itself
Contributor dynamics show healthy project growth, with new contributors displacing older code through refactoring and bug fixes
money_quotes: Your existing i3 configuration file will work as-is on Sway, and your keybindings and colors and fonts and for_window rules and so on will all be the same.
Today it seems that most of the features sway needs are implemented.
You can donate to the features you want to see, and you can claim the donations by implementing the features and sending a pull request.
Not every feature is for sway - anything that improves the sway experience is eligible for a bounty, and in fact over half of the initial bounties are for features in other parts of the ecosystem.
Lots of the top contributors are slowly having their lines of code reduced as lots of new contributors are coming in and displacing them with refactorings and bug fixes.
key_insight: Sway has reached feature maturity as a Wayland i3 replacement, and is now shifting focus from implementation to ecosystem growth through community funding and contributor incentives.
